steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Recognize the standard form of a circle
steps.content: The equation is in the standard form of a circle, where the right side equals $r^2$ (the square of the radius), not the radius itself.

steps.title: Calculate the radius
steps.content:
We have $r^2 = 81$. Taking the square root of both sides:
$$r = \sqrt{81} = 9$$
That is the correct answer.
